data.title: UBS says ‘underweight the names with high China exposure’
data.description:
U.S. names with China exposure face weaker growth, wrote Andrew Garthwaite, chief global equity strategist at UBS, in his Outlook 2025 report.

He said that there are three general threats to China-exposed stocks:

  • Weaker growth: about 3.7% in 2025 and 2.7% in 2026.
  • A weaker renminbi (7.60 by end 2025, analysts said)
  • The threat of retaliatory tariffs on Western products.

UBS listed neutral U.S. stocks with China exposure.

“The problem being that this time around, unlike 2018, U.S. tariffs on China look to be much larger and thus harder to offset with currency depreciation (for the renminbi) and are more likely to lead China to seek to redirect its exports elsewhere,” said Garthwaite.

“This could lead say to Europe imposing tariffs on Chinese products in response, which in turn leads to the risk of retaliatory tariffs by China,” he added.

Also, for the consumer names, there is the risk of buying boycotts from China to U.S. or Western products in response to the tariffs, he said.

U.S. companies with high China exposure:

  • Nike (NKE) – China exposure: 15%
  • Lululemon Athletica (LULU) – China exposure: 10%
  • Agilent Technologies (A) – China exposure: 20%
  • Otis Worldwide Corp. (OTIS) – China exposure: 17%
  • MGM Resorts Int. (MGM) – China exposure: 20%
  • Apple (AAPL) – China exposure: 19%
  • Revvity (RVTY) – China exposure: 17%
  • Viatris (VTRS) – China exposure: 14%
  • Western Digital (WDC) – China exposure: 23%
  • Ebay (EBAY) – China exposure: 10%

data.title: M&A intrigue: Soho House trades 18% below its takeover offer price
data.description:
Soho House & Co (NYSE:SHCO) is being sized up by investors, a day after the company confirmed it received an acquisition offer at $9.00 per share from a third-party consortium. The offer is conditioned on certain significant shareholders, including Executive Chairman Ron Burkle, and The Yucaipa Companies, rolling over their equity interests in the company as part of the transaction.

Roth MKM kept a Buy rating on Soho House (SHCO). Analyst George Kelly said with the stock closing on Thursday well below the $9 offer, the market is clearly skeptical of the buyout deal due to a lack of details in respect to the consortium makeup and the additional conditional shareholders, who too were not made public. "We wonder if the consortium is associated with Burkle or Yucaipa, or if they may not be well received by SHCO's member base," Kelly asked.

Kelly thinks SHCO is an attractive asset and that a transaction is likely down the road with "motivated" large shareholders in the mix.

Of note, Soho House's (SHCO) board turned down an offer last year from an unspecified bidder.

Soho House (SHCO) traded 5.7% higher on Friday after a huge rally on Thursday, Shares are swapping hands at $7.63, which is about 18% below the deal price.

data.title: J.P. Morgan’s top Technology stocks for 2025
data.description:
J.P. Morgan’s U.S. equity analysts picked their top stocks for 2025, selecting “the most compelling investment ideas” across every sector.

These are the technology stocks (NYSEARCA:XLK) highlighted:

Aramark (ARMK) – Price target 45.00

J.P. Morgan analysts said they have “been impressed by Aramark’s (ARMK) execution, absent two large contract losses in 2024 that we view as highly idiosyncratic.”

They expect the company’s base revenues to continue to benefit from increasing automation and self-checkout penetration. “These technologies help drive transaction volumes and allow for a wider array of service formats.”

The company “also possesses clear paths ahead for both margin expansion and financial deleveraging… [trading] at 20x 2025E EPS, versus 25x for best-in-class peer Compass Group (OTCPK:CMPGF).”

Broadcom (AVGO) – Price target: 250.00

“The AI infrastructure build-out driving is strong demand for Broadcom’s (AVGO) ASIC (application-specific integrated circuit)/networking solutions,” analysts said.

AI revenues this year were $12.2B, up 3x year-over-year. “We believe [Broadcom is] set to grow to $17-18B (up 40% year-over-year) in 2025 given continued strong cloud/hyperscaler capex spending trends with focus on AI infrastructure build-out and ramp of Google’s next-gen TPU v6 3nm AI accelerator ASIC.”

CyberArk (CYBR) – Price target: 375.00

“While the company continues to expand outside its core PAM (potential available markets), CyberArk (CYBR) remains one of the best-positioned vendors within our coverage with an improving growth profile and expanding margins,” analysts said.

Dell Technologies (DELL) – Price target: 160.00

“We anticipate a broadening of demand drivers to include Traditional IT Infrastructure in 2025, moving beyond the focused investments in AI seen in 2024, which situates Dell (DELL) as one of the best-positioned companies within our coverage, due to materially stronger core business growth.”

Analysts estimate the earnings potential from Traditional Infrastructure demand, assuming a normal spending environment, to be about $9 per share and project about $2 per share of earnings potential from AI Infrastructure “which supports a bull-case valuation that is materially higher than the current share price.”

Guidewire Software (GWRE) – Price target: 228.00

“Guidewire (GWRE) holds a dominant competitive position among larger insurers, with customers highlighting its robust product roadmap and strong execution as key advantages, enabling the company to command premium pricing,” analysts said.

The company is “well-positioned for accelerated growth heading into 2025E.”

“Management is increasingly confident that subscription and support gross margins could eventually exceed 80%,” J.P. Morgan added. “While Guidewire (GWRE) may maintain substantial investments in its platform, product development, and third-party partnerships, which could limit operating leverage, we believe the company is well-positioned for long-term growth and profitability improvements.”

HubSpot (HUBS) – Price target: 635.00

“Any improvement in the small business environment and/or resumption of front-office investments could serve as positive developments for HubSpot (HUBS), while it is also benefiting from a price increase that is going into effect.”

KLA Corp. (KLAC) – Price target: 950.00

Analysts said that “major current and new technology inflections, combined with new product introductions, Actinic mask inspection, new metrology tools for 3D structures, and enhanced software solutions should drive a strong, multi-year runway of demand for KLA’s process control, packaging, and specialty semiconductor systems.”

Joint Stock Co. Kaspi.kz (OTC:KSPI) – Price target: 137.00

“Fundamentals and Kaspi’s competitive advantages remain as strong as ever, and we think there is low-hanging fruit on the investor messaging side to improve sentiment,” analysts said.

Shares trade 8x 2025 earnings despite a greater than 20% revenue growth and a greater than 40% net income margins.

Mastercard (MA) – Price target: 590.00

“Already-identified pricing should support stable 2025 growth with upside if Mastercard (MA) implements further tokenization pricing (particularly in U.S. market),” analysts said. “We see absolute valuation upside for both networks, particularly Visa (V) as it has a ceiling to raise tokenization pricing, which could create a rising tide to both network’s multiples.”

Mondays.com (MNDY) – Price target: 350.00

“The company has executed well despite high expectations from investors, driving a 34% year-over-year last-twelve-month revenue growth rate, at a more than $1B revenue rate, while producing a 31% free cash flow margin, despite a choppy macro backdrop.”

In addition, analyst believe that “the company presents a unique opportunity as it breaks out of the mold of collaborative work management into a multi-product story, with an edge to enable low-code, no-code business workflow orchestration, while driving a strong profitable growth glide path at a considerable scale.”

Synopsys Inc. (SNPS) – Price target: 685.00

“We believe the company’s recent 2025 guidance includes several areas of conservatism/de-risking and sets up for potential upward revisions.”

Also, “semiconductor chip design activity continues to be strong and driving incremental demand for more/better chip design software/hardware tools,” analysts said. “We continue to see the team driving low-double-digit percentage revenue CAGR over the next several years.”

TransUnion (TRU) – Price target: 124.00

“The valuation on TransUnion (TRU) shares at 24x 2025 EPS remains below peer Equifax (EFX) at 33x and the information services sector median of 30x,” J.P. Morgan analysts said.

“Historically, TransUnion (TRU) had traded above Equifax (EFX) and the sector’s median given [its] higher organic, constant currency revenue growth profile… We think the stock will continue to re-rate higher as long as the team continues to deliver against expectations.”

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow soar 2% each as soft inflation data helps overcome Fed 'overreaction'
data.description:
Wall Street surged on Friday, after some favorable inflation data helped traders look past the Federal Reserve's hawkish tilt that roiled markets two days ago.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +1.74% at 5,969.43 points in midday trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.77% at 19,715.82 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +1.84% at 43,119.35 points.

All 11 S&P sectors advanced, led by Real Estate and Technology.

A spree of bond-buying sent U.S. Treasury yields lower. The 10-year yield (US10Y) snapped a two-day selloff, slipping 9 basis points to 4.49%. The shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) shed 3 basis points to 4.30%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

U.S. stocks tried to rebound in Thursday's session following Wednesday's Fed-inspired carnage. However, due to a lack of any major catalyst for positive sentiment, the attempt largely fizzled out.

Friday's economic calendar provided the needed spark. Before the opening bell, market participants received U.S. personal income and outlays data for November. That report contained an update on the core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index - widely seen as the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. In November, the core PCE rose 0.1% M/M, a slower pace than the consensus of +0.2% and October's gain of 0.3%.

"If Fed policymakers were making wishes, they'd want services spending to cool, goods spending to pick up and for all of this to happen with inflation coming down and a labor market strong enough to sustain income growth. Today's personal income and spending report largely granted those wishes," Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan said.

The Fed on Wednesday had cited inflation as one of its primary reasons for forecasting a slower pace of interest rate cuts. Fed chair Jerome Powell even went on to say that policymakers' year-end projection for inflation had "fallen apart" into the end of 2024. Following such messaging, today's soft core PCE deflator reading came as a balm to investors.

Princeton economics professor and former Fed vice chair Alan Blinder called the Fed-induced selling a "way, way overreaction."

"Many years ago, I coined the aphorism that in calm markets, they generally react three times as much as they should, and in panicky markets they react ten times as much. This looked like a ten to me," Blinder said in a Bloomberg TV interview on Wednesday.

On Friday, politics grabbed a chunk of the spotlight. A stopgap spending plan backed by President-elect Donald Trump was rejected in the House of Representatives, further fueling concerns of a government shutdown. Trump called for a removal of the debt ceiling.

The latest bill was voted down by 235 votes to 174. The funding is set to expire on Friday night.

"A short-lived shutdown affects government workers, but has limited economic impact. The longer a shutdown lasts, the more disruptive it is to the U.S. economy. Economic data may not get published in a shutdown," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Turning to Friday's active stocks, U.S.-listed shares of Novo Nordisk (NVO) slumped -17%, after the Danish drugmaker announced disappointing results from a late-stage trial of its next-gen weight loss therapy, CagriSema. Rival Eli Lilly (LLY) climbed +5%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

More on the markets

  • We Are Carving Out A Bottom, Don't Panic
  • 2025 Markets To Be Driven By Liquidity, Lobbying And Collectibility - Most Likely
  • Canadian Banks Are Seeing Increased Risks
  • As Trump aims to slash federal spending, Wells Fargo economists see no major cuts in 2025
  • J.P. Morgan’s top Financial stocks for 2025

data.title: MannKind stock up 10% amid bullish Wells Fargo note
data.description:
Shares of MannKind (NASDAQ:MNKD) were up 10% in midday trading Friday after Wells Fargo initiated coverage of the stock with an overweight rating and a price target of $9.

The investment bank said in a note that it sees MannKind’s (NASDAQ:MNKD) lung drug candidates MNKD-101 and MNKD-201 moving into the spotlight in 2025/2026.

Wells said that while many investors probably view the company as a “Tyvaso DPI royalty plus Afrezza revenue story,” MNKD-101 in NTM lung disease and MNKD-201 in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis “are under the radar programs with potential opportunities that are multiples of what the current base business is generating today.”

data.title: Brinker International deserves credit for Chili's turnaround, earning upgrade at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley upgraded Brinker International (NYSE:EAT) to Equal Weight from Underweight and lifted the price target by 64% to $115 to account for the stock’s recent advance and data suggesting the company continues to perform very well.

Brinker (EAT) shares have appreciated 215% since last year, outperforming the S&P 500 by more than 9 times.

“We’re less concerned about reversion, lack of flow through, and broader P&L/balance sheet than we were,” Morgan Stanley’s Brian Harbour said, with the company deserving credit for the Chili’s turnaround.

Harbour is modeling 20% growth in Chili’s same store sales in the fiscal second quarter with upside possible depending on how December turns out, and a bump in his full year EPS estimates assuming flow through can remain generally solid. His EPS estimate is now $6.74 for FY25, from $5.45, and $7.87 for FY26 from $5.93.

Relying on casual dining metrics and the company’s performance, Harbour doesn’t see the upward estimate revision cycle ending soon for Brinker (EAT) and is increasingly sold on its durability. He models free cash flow of ~$280M this year and growing, along with strong cash flow which will enable more capital return.

“[Brinker International] remains one of the more interesting stories, and asked about stocks, in our coverage into CY25, and worth watching,” Harbour adds.

Shares are up 2% at midday with comparable gains for Dine Brands (DIN), Darden Restaurants (DRI), and Restaurant Brands International (QSR).

data.title: DexCom, Insulet, Tandem benefit from Novo Nordisk CagriSema data
data.description:
• Insulin pump makers DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM), Insulet (NASDAQ:PODD), and Tandem Diabetes Care (NASDAQ:TNDM) are all markedly up in Friday trading as beneficiaries of disappointing data for Novo Nordisk's (NVO) CagriSema for weight loss.
  • The reason is that the data, which missed company expectations, could been a boon for diabetes technology players.
  • Leerink's Mike Kratky wrote that the data could lead to a relief rally for companies like DexCom, Insulet, and Tandem, which are up, respectively, ~7%, ~4.6%, and ~5%, on the day so far.
  • Year to date, Insulet and Tandem are up double digits percentage wise, though DexCom is down ~35%.

data.title: J.P. Morgan’s top Financial stocks for 2025
data.description:
J.P. Morgan’s U.S. equity analysts picked their top stocks for 2025, selecting “the most compelling investment ideas” across every sector.

These are the financial stocks (NYSEARCA:XLF) highlighted:

Allstate (ALL) – Price target: 217.00

Analysts expect Allstate’s (ALL) personal auto and home margins to continue to expand as insurance price hikes and cost cuts offset higher marketing spending.

“Margins should benefit from previous price hikes flowing through results, incremental price increases implemented by management, moderating inflation, and controlled G&A (general and administrative) growth.”

In addition, although the stock’s valuation has recovered, “it still trades at a depressed multiple on normalized margins and income,” analysts said. “We view further improvement in personal lines, a turn in PIF (product information file) growth, and the resumption of share repurchases as potential positive catalysts.”

Globe Life (GL) – Price target 136.00

The stock’s valuation has recovered, “but we feel that it still offers significant upside potential.”

Analysts “forecast Globe Life’s (GL) niche market focus, low-cost platform, and steady capital deployment to drive a mid-teens ROE and high-single-digit EPS growth over time.”

Also, “we believe the company is more defensively positioned than peers in an adverse macro scenario given its lower allocation to other higher-risk investments, below-average asset leverage, and lack of equity exposure.”

OneMain Holdings (OMF) – Price target: 59.00

The company is levered to credit support from potential immigration policies, “which could reduce labor supply for blue collar jobs, driving higher wage growth, slower rent growth, and lower gas prices,” J.P. Morgan analysts said.

“All of these are a tailwind to originations growth, disposable income, and credit performance for OneMain’s (OMF) target market,” analysts said. “Should the outlook improve, [OneMain] may loosen underwriting standards and accelerate loan growth.”

PNC Financial (PNC) – Price target: 206.50

The bank “should benefit in net interest income from fixed asset and swaps repricing, even with uncertainty about rates.”

It “should also hold up relatively better if the economy slows given its more conservative credit risk profile.”

Regency Centers (REG) – Price target: 80.00

“We think the company is poised to generate about 3%+ SS NOI (same store net operating income) growth for the year as leased occupancy translates into physical occupancy gains, and it should see redevelopment contributions in 2025 and development impact in 2026.”

Charles Schwab (SCHW) – Price target: 87.00

Analysts see potential for the company to outperform its peers next year.

“In a market where well-regarded companies in attractive sectors have experienced meaningful multiple expansion in 2024, and where low growth businesses in less attractive sectors have seen valuations stall if not contract, we see Schwab (SCHW) in a position to better approach more normalized earnings as it pays down particularly high-cost short term borrowing that could push NIM (net interest margin) towards 300 basis points with only a moderate decline in the size of its balance sheet.”

Western Alliance Bancorp (WAL) – Price target: 110.00

Western Alliance (WAL) “stands to benefit from a lower rate environment tied to its ECR (earnings credit rate) deposit costs likely declining in the fourth quarter of 2024, as well as 2025,” analysts said.

“With the Fed also likely to continue reducing the funds rate in 2025 at a measured pace, this should serve as a tailwind to ECR deposit costs declining in 2025, thereby providing a tailwind to NIM expansion in 2025.”

data.title: Rates can still go down 'a fair amount,' Chicago Fed's Goolsbee says
data.description:
A "little more uncertainty" and noise in recent economic data prompted Chicago Fed President Austin Goolsbee to make his projected interest rate path for 2025 a little more shallow, he said on Friday in a TV interview.

The better-than-expected November PCE data that came out Friday morning, though, makes him hopeful that inflation firming in the prior couple of months were "more of a bump" than a change in trend.

"I've been saying that the overall thread is that inflation is way down. I believe we're on the path to 2%, and over the next 12 to 18 months, rates can still go down a fair amount," he said in an interview on CNBC. "Whether that happens three months earlier, or three months later, I don't think is the most material thing."

The "material thing" is that inflation has come down, he explained, adding "we need to keep making progress."

The economy has reached "stable full employment," he said. "We want to keep that stabilized, and the way to do that is to have the rates come down to something like neutral."

Earlier in the day, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said she voted against a rate cut on Wednesday because she sees risks skewing more toward the inflation side of the Fed's dual mandate.

data.title: Guggenheim starts bullishly on Reddit on growth potential going ahead
data.description:
Analysts at Guggenheim have initiated Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) with a "buy" rating on Friday. They believe the company is poised to capitalize on strong social media growth in the quarters and years ahead.

Shares of the company are up more than 5% before midday on the NYSE

The research firm said growth for the company would be driven by a highly engaged global user base, significant ad monetization improvement, and expanding opportunities in early-stage initiatives like search, data licensing, commerce, and expanded formats.

"Consumer interest in Reddit is strong and growing, with the daily user base growing 47% over the past 12 months and searches for Reddit exiting the year at an all-time high. Comparative financial analysis indicates significant monetization opportunity on a per daily user basis," the research firm said Friday.

Going into 2025, Guggenheim expects investor interest in the the company to remain high, it sees consumer engagement continuing to grow. They also forecast results to meaningfully outperform current consensus estimates.

RDDT has a PT of $210, implying an upside of over 28%. Stock has gained nearly 5x its value since going public in March, while the benchmark S&P index rose 12.3%.
